welcome.subject: Welcome to Doxa! - {campaign}
welcome.doxaMeaning: In Greek, doxa means "glory," and that is exactly what we are offering as we join in prayer on behalf of the millions of people who live with little to no gospel witness. Together, we are bringing glory to God through faithful, expectant prayer.
welcome.reminderExplanation: As part of this prayer campaign, you will soon begin receiving reminder emails with links to daily prayer content. Each link will include guided prayer prompts and other materials to help you pray strategically and faithfully for your chosen unengaged people group.
welcome.sharedGoal: Through this movement, our shared goal is to see 100+ people praying daily for each of the over 2,000 unengaged people groups, men and women sent to engage these people groups with the gospel by the year 2033, and sustained giving to support this effort.
welcome.closing: We are deeply grateful to join with the whole Church as we seek to take the whole gospel to the whole world.
welcome.reminderTimeNote: Your daily reminder is set for {time}.
welcome.profileInstructions: You can update your profile details and your notifications here:
welcome.addToCalendar: Add this reminder to your calendar:

reminder.subject: Prayer Reminder - {campaign}
reminder.timeForPrayer: It's time for your {duration}-minute prayer session.
reminder.automatedReminder: This is an automated prayer reminder from {appName}.

followup.standardIntro: We'd love to hear how your prayer journey is going!
followup.feedbackHelps: Your feedback helps us understand how we can better support your prayer journey.
followup.pauseNotice: If we don't hear from you, we'll stop your prayer reminders to avoid filling your inbox. You can always start them again from your profile.
followup.commitmentCheckIn: This is a commitment check-in from {appName}.

adoptionVerification.ignoreMessage: If you didn't submit an adoption form, you can safely ignore this email.

adoptionWelcome.subject: DOXA Adoption Update: Your Commitment to the {peopleGroupName}
adoptionWelcome.intro: Today, as part of the DOXA partnership, you changed the status of the {peopleGroupName} from [waiting] to [adopted], the vital first step to engagement.
adoptionWelcome.introContinued: You are now a primary advocate, intercessor, and mobilizer for a people group who, until this moment, had no consistent witness or dedicated prayer effort.
adoptionWelcome.resourcesIntro: The following resources will help you steward this opportunity.
adoptionWelcome.section2Intro: We have put together a number of resources specific to your people group to help you mobilize and share quickly and easily about this initiative. You will find:
adoptionWelcome.section1Item1: URL and QR code to your people group, tips, answers to frequently asked questions, and more
adoptionWelcome.section1Item2: Images to help you introduce this to your church or small group
adoptionWelcome.section1Item3: A dedicated link to make it easy to contribute financially to the DOXA Foundation
adoptionWelcome.fullProfileText: Review details about your people group by visiting their
adoptionWelcome.prayerPageText: Mobilize people to pray through this
adoptionWelcome.section3Intro: Here are our recommended next action steps:
adoptionWelcome.step1Text: Pray 10 minutes daily for the {peopleGroupName}. Ask God to give you a heart for this place and these people. Ask Him to give you wisdom for the next steps and favor with all who will hear because of your efforts to share the vision. Ask Him to prepare hearts. Expect Him to answer — He is already at work! Start by visiting their {prayerPageLink}.
adoptionWelcome.step2Text: Make a plan with your church leadership on how to introduce this to your church and community. Share your digital media and other resources found in your {adoptionResourcesLink} with your church family and on your social platforms. Let people know that this group is your focus.
adoptionWelcome.step3Text: Identify 5 people who will sign up to pray with you. Consider prayer leaders, small group members, and spiritually faithful friends who are likely to be aligned in priorities and vision. A small core in sustained, expectant agreement can be the beginning of a movement.
adoptionWelcome.section4Text: Every month, you will receive a strategic monthly update — briefing you on any regional breakthroughs, new resources, or mobilization milestones related to your group. If you have questions, need encouragement, or help thinking through next steps, we want to help. Please reach out to {contactEmail}.
adoptionWelcome.closingCount: There were {beforeCount} groups left to adopt. Because of your "yes" today, that number is now {afterCount}. We are grateful to partner with you in this worthy endeavor.

autoAck.subject: We received your message
autoAck.body: Thanks for reaching out to {appName}. We have received your message and a member of our team will get back to you soon.

ai.uncertainty: Check these — not found in our material
ai.unavailable: AI is busy right now — try again in a moment
ai.replaceMessage: Drafting with AI will replace the text currently in the reply box.
ai.directionPlaceholder: Optional: steer this draft — e.g. "Ask them to sign up for a people group in their country and link to their country's page."
ai.emptyHint: Optionally steer the draft above, then click Generate.

kb.reviewHint: Review and edit this anonymised question and answer before saving. It will help ground future AI drafts.
kb.question: Question
kb.pageSubtitle: Anonymised answers captured from real conversations. Used to ground AI draft replies.
kb.empty: No knowledge entries yet. Use "Add to knowledge base" on a resolved conversation to capture one.
kb.refreshedPartial: Refreshed {n} pages — {m} failed and kept their old content
kb.deleteMessage: This permanently deletes the entry. It will no longer ground future AI drafts.

page.title: Your prayer experience
page.intro: Thank you for taking a few minutes to help us shape future daily prayer content. There are no right or wrong answers — we just want your honest experience.
page.thanks: Thank you — your responses are in. We're grateful for the time you took, and for your prayers.
page.editNotice: You've already responded — thank you! You can update your answers below and resave if you'd like.
page.invalid: This survey link doesn't look valid. Please use the link from your email, or contact us if you need help.
page.closed: This survey is no longer accepting responses. Thank you for your interest.
page.error: We couldn't save your responses. Please try again.

frequency.label: How many days per week do you typically use the curated prayer content provided by DOXA?

focus.label: The daily prayer prompts help me focus and engage in praying for unreached peoples.

experience.label: Tell us more about your experience — what part of the daily prayer page has been most helpful to you, and why?

clarity.label: The prayer prompts clearly communicate what to pray for and why it is important.

balance.label: The mix of Scripture, prayer points, and reflection feels balanced for an approximately 10-minute prayer time.

scripted_preference.label: The prayer content contains both prayer points with strategic suggestions for how to pray (i.e. Please pray that God would raise up missionaries from all over the world to plant the Church together among unreached people groups.) and scripted prayers (i.e. Father, today we pray that this people group would experience the promise you made to Abraham that they would be blessed through him. Amen.). Would you prefer:

options.2: About half the prayer requests be scripted
options.3: Only one prayer request be scripted (current format)

new_pg_daily.label: Do you enjoy the section encouraging you to pray for a different, unengaged people group each day in addition to your specific people group?

background_info.label: Is it helpful to see the background information on your people group at the bottom of the prayer content each day?

improvement.label: If we could improve one thing about future daily content, what would you change, add, or remove?

email.subject: Would you share how prayer has been going?
email.p1: It has been a privilege to join with you in intercession for the unreached peoples of the world.
email.p2: We're always trying to make the daily prayer prompts more helpful, and before we meet as a team to plan what's next, we'd love to hear from you. Would you take a few minutes to answer a few short questions about your experience?
email.p3: Your honest feedback — what's working, what isn't — directly shapes the prayer content we create.
